response.justification: Sarah Smith is a capable full-stack and distributed-systems engineer, but her profile fundamentally misaligns with the core requirements of this role. C++ experience is minimal — roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis project — and is conspicuously absent from her own skills summary. There is no Rust experience. While she founded GISCollective and built a web GIS application for Green Map, this is hobbyist and NGO-grade geospatial work rather than production routing pipelines, tiling systems, or navigation guidance at scale. Her decade-plus of commercial work is dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, and web technologies, making her a poor fit for a C++-centric geospatial data pipeline role.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS domain interest demonstrated through GISCollective platform and Green Map web GIS application
Distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic — microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O
Strong software-craftsmanship fundamentals (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) applicable to any engineering environment
concerns: C++ experience is superficial and over a decade old; absent from her own skills summary entirely
No Rust experience and no commercial systems-programming work beyond a brief six-month C/C++ role in 2014–2015
GIS work is web-layer and hobbyist in nature — no exposure to routing tile pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or real-time traffic data integration

key_factors.factor: Geospatial domain interest and GIS exposure
